evals.name: react-app-setup
evals.prompt: I want to add Peril to my React app so my team can annotate bugs in the UI and my agent can pick them up. Can you set it up?
evals.expected_output: Installs @peril packages, wraps App with ReviewProvider, adds .peril/ to .gitignore, configures MCP, adds review mode toggle

assertions.text: ReviewProvider has serverUrl prop pointing to the Peril backend
An MCP configuration file is created for an agent
A review mode toggle is added using useReviewMode hook

evals.name: mcp-config-only
evals.prompt: I already have @peril-ai/react installed and working, but I need to configure the MCP server so Cursor can see the reviews. Can you set that up?
evals.expected_output: Creates .cursor/mcp.json with peril MCP config, explains how to start peril-mcp, does not reinstall packages or modify React code

assertions.text: MCP config contains a 'peril' server entry with npx @peril-ai/mcp
Does NOT modify App.tsx since it's already configured
Does NOT run npm install since packages are already installed

evals.name: vanilla-js-setup
evals.prompt: I have a vanilla JS app built with Vite. I want to add Peril review annotations. I don't use React.
evals.expected_output: Installs @peril-ai/core (not @peril-ai/react), uses createReviewOverlay from core, configures server and MCP

assertions.text: Uses createReviewOverlay from @peril-ai/core to set up the overlay
An MCP configuration file is created
No files import from @peril-ai/react or use ReviewProvider
